2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch introduces a new callback function called ->iomap_next() in the iomap API. This function replaces the existing ->iomap_begin() and ->iomap_end() callbacks, allowing for more efficient iteration over mappings. The ->iomap_next() callback is responsible for finishing the previous mapping if necessary and producing the next mapping. This change enables performance-critical callers to inline their iteration loop and pass their ->iomap_next() function as a compile-time constant, which can be devirtualized by the compiler into a direct call instead of an indirect call through a function pointer. The patch also updates several files to use the new callback function.

reviews.author: Christoph Hellwig
reviews.summary: Reviewed the core iomap bits and provided a Reviewed-by tag. However, expressed reservations about the simple dio part of the patch and offered to post an alternative version.
sentiment_signals: no clear opinion on the patch
reviews.raw_body:
As mentioned in the coverletter not a fan of the simple dio part
of this, but I'll post a version of my patch for that that slots
in before this.  For the core iomap bits:

Reviewed-by: Christoph Hellwig <hch@lst.de>

2026-07-22.patch_summary: This patch introduces a new ->iomap_next() callback in the iomap API, which replaces the existing ->iomap_begin() and ->iomap_end() callbacks. The goal is to improve performance by allowing callers to inline their iteration loop and pass ->iomap_next() as a compile-time constant.
